response:
Nurse Emily has organized a creative writing workshop for 24 residents, dividing them into 4 groups of 6. Each group will receive 3 writing prompts initially, totaling 12 prompts per group. Additionally, Emily has prepared 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. 

The initial prompts will be distributed as 3 per group, and the extra prompts will be divided equally among the 4 groups. This means each group will receive 2 extra prompts. Therefore, the total number of prompts will be 12 + 2 = 14 per group, multiplied by 4 groups, resulting in 56 prompts in total.

The library of writing prompts is essential for the success of the workshop, and the collection of prompts must be well-curated to support the needs of the older adults. The library should be organized in a way that ensures accessibility and relevance for all participants.

The library should be thoroughly reviewed to ensure that the prompts are appropriate and engaging for the target audience. The library is a vital resource that supports the therapeutic value of the workshop.

The END.
